mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations and stimulates corporate borrowing, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate decline, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing equity risk premiums to widen and S&P 500 returns to turn negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real personal income and industrial production confirms synchronized demand and supply expansion, validating earnings growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and INDPRO diverge, or walk-forward Sharpe ratio of the strategy falls below 0.5 over 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation, rising CPIAUCSL forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >50% of high-inflation months, or the predictive relationship reverses when inflation expectations become anchored below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which directly supports earnings revisions and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in >55% of months following industrial production growth, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts drop below 1000 thousand units, construction activity and related consumer spending contract sharply, signaling a demand shock that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when HOUST remains below 1000.0 for consecutive months, or forward returns show no statistical significance with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms a broad-based economic expansion, reducing default risk and boosting corporate profit margins, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when labor productivity decouples from output growth, or the strategy yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 3-year rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market with elevated price levels, the combination of high CPIAUCSL and low UNRATE triggers aggressive monetary tightening expectations, rapidly increasing discount rates and forcing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets remain flat or positive during overheating episodes, or the joint threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with a Rank IC above 0.04.
